# see — static app share service

`see` hosts uploaded static apps in a sandboxed viewer. Bundles extend the basic share with a `see.json` manifest that declares settings and static CSS-variable tweaks.

---

## Bundles (recommended)

A **bundle** is the first-class way to ship an app to `see`: your static files (html, css, images, svg, json, …) **plus a root `see.json` manifest**. The manifest is the single source of truth for everything the platform offers — no hand-wiring in your HTML. Upload a zip or file set containing `see.json` at the root and the share is automatically a bundle. "Settings" is just editing the manifest.

### `see.json`

```json
{
  "homepage": "index.html",
  "exposed": ["index.html", "pricing.html"],
  "bar": true,
  "tweaks": {
    "primaryColor": { "kind": "color",  "value": "#D97757", "cssVar": "--color-primary", "label": "Primary", "group": "Theme" },
    "fontSize":     { "kind": "number", "value": 16, "min": 12, "max": 24, "step": 1, "unit": "px", "cssVar": "--font-size-base", "label": "Font size" }
  },
  "pages": {
    "pricing.html": {
      "tweaks": {
        "primaryColor": { "value": "#0A84FF" },
        "showBadge":    { "kind": "text", "value": "inline", "cssVar": "--badge-display", "label": "Sale badge" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

| field | meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `homepage` | resource path served at the share root (existing HTML page) |
| `exposed` | HTML pages the viewer offers as navigable entry points (each must exist) |
| `bar` | default visibility of the viewer bar (boolean) |
| `tweaks` | named design knobs applied as static CSS custom properties — the **shared** defaults inherited by every page |
| `pages` | optional per-page tweak overrides, keyed by page resource path (see below) |

**Static CSS-variable tweaks.** Each entry is `"<id>": { "kind": …, "value": …, "cssVar": "--…", "unit": "px"?, "label": …, "group": … }`.

- **Only tweaks with a `cssVar` take effect.** For each, the content handler injects a single static `<style>:root { --cssVar: value; }</style>` into served HTML — zero page JS, no SDK. Tweaks without `cssVar` are ignored.
- `kind` is `"toggle"` | `"number"` | `"color"` | `"text"` | `"select"` (inferred from `value` type if omitted). Use a `"text"` tweak when the CSS value is a string, e.g. `--badge-display: inline`.
- Other optional fields: `label`, `group`, `min`, `max`, `step`, `unit`, `options` (for `select`).

Your CSS just consumes `var(--color-primary)` and the tweak works with no page code.

**Per-page tweaks (inheritance).** Root `tweaks` are shared defaults inherited by every page. The top-level `pages` map adds per-page overrides keyed by page path (`"index.html"`, `"pricing.html"`):

```
"pages": { "<page-path>": { "tweaks": { "<id>": <partial tweak> } } }
```

- A page tweak entry is a **partial**: override just the value (`{ "value": "#0A84FF" }` — inherits `cssVar`/metadata from the shared tweak of the same id), define a brand-new page-only knob, or override only metadata.
- **Resolution = page layer over shared, merged per field.** Effective def is `{ ...shared, ...page }`; effective value is the page value if set, else shared. Injected as the same static `<style>:root{…}</style>`.
- Each `pages` key must be an existing HTML page (validated on upload/patch).

Edit a shared value via pointer `/tweaks/<id>/value`; edit a page's own value via `/pages/<page-path>/tweaks/<id>/value`. These are **separate** surfaces — a shared edit never touches a page layer and vice versa. In a JSON Pointer, `/` inside a page path is escaped as `~1` (RFC 6901), e.g. `sub/page.html` → `/pages/sub~1page.html/tweaks/<id>/value`. The patch engine auto-creates missing parents, so these pointers work even before any `pages` node exists.

### Editing a bundle = patching `see.json`

Edit `see.json` via the viewer's settings resource editor or the structured patch API (below) — it's plain JSON, so address any node with a JSON pointer:

```json
{ "ops": [
  { "file": "see.json", "pointer": "/tweaks/primaryColor/value", "action": "set", "value": "#0A84FF" },
  { "file": "see.json", "pointer": "/exposed/-",                  "action": "append", "value": "pricing.html" }
] }
```

An invalid manifest is rejected loudly (422 `invalid_manifest` on patch, 400 on upload/replace) and nothing is written. The Settings endpoint only accepts `password`; homepage/exposed/bar/tweaks/pages are owned by `see.json`.

---

## Editing content (for agents)

A share is a living set of files — patch them over time and every open viewer reloads automatically. Origin = the host serving this `/llms.txt`.

**Where content is served (verify your changes here):**
- `GET /content/{id}/` — the **rendered app** at the homepage, with tweaks injected. A specific page is `GET /content/{id}/<path>`.
- `GET /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — the **RAW stored file** (no injected `<style>`).

To confirm a tweak/edit took effect, fetch `/content/{id}/…` — the raw resource won't show the injected `<style>`.

**File management:**
- `GET /api/uploads/{id}/resources` — list files (`{ resources: [{ path, bytes, contentType, … }], revision }`).
- `GET /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — read one raw file (before patching).
- `POST /api/uploads/{id}/patch` — structured surgical patching (preferred — see below).
- `PATCH /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — replace one whole file (raw body).
- `POST /api/uploads/{id}/resources` — add/replace via multipart (`file`, optional `path`).
- `DELETE /api/uploads/{id}/resources/{path}` — remove a file (an index page must remain).
- `POST /api/uploads/{id}/claim` — rename to a memorable name. Body `{ "name": "my-cool-demo" }` (slugified). New id is `<name>-<suffix>`; response returns `{ id, viewerUrl, contentUrl, revision }`. The old id stops resolving — use the returned id afterward.

Mutations bump the revision and live-reload open viewers; you need do nothing special for that. Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <password>` if the share has a password. If the owner cleared it, edits are open (the link alone grants editing).

### Structured patching — `POST /api/uploads/{id}/patch`

Address exact nodes with the query language native to the file type (detected from extension) and batch many edits into one request:

```json
{
  "dryRun": false,
  "ops": [
    { "file": "index.html", "select": ".hero h1", "action": "setText", "value": "Summer Sale" },
    { "file": "data.json",  "pointer": "/items/0/price", "action": "set", "value": 42 }
  ]
}
```

Response: `{ ok, dryRun, revision, changed: [...], results: [{ file, matched, applied, error? }, …] }`, one entry per op in order; `matched` is how many nodes the address hit (a selector may match several — the op applies to all). The batch is atomic: any invalid op rejects the whole thing with 422 and nothing is written; a selector matching zero nodes is not an error (`matched: 0`, skipped). Set `dryRun: true` to get match counts without writing. Max 200 ops.

Actions by file type:
- **HTML** (`.html`/`.htm`, `select` = CSS selector): `setText`/`setHtml`/`replaceWith`/`append`/`prepend` (`value`), `setAttr` (`name`,`value`), `removeAttr` (`name`), `addClass`/`removeClass` (`value`), `remove`.
- **JSON** (`.json`, `pointer` = RFC 6901; `""` is the whole doc): `set` (`value`; trailing `/-` appends to an array), `insert` (`value`, at index), `append` (`value`, push), `remove`.
- **CSS** (`.css`, `selectRule` = rule selector text): `setDecl` (`prop`,`value`; adds if absent), `removeDecl` (`prop`), `removeRule`, `addRule` (`selector`, `declarations` object).
